title: rhamnus occidentalis
intro: Rhamnus occidentalis, also known as the western poison ivy oak, is a shrub native to the western coastal regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rhamnus occidentalis include treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis, as well as reducing fever and relieving pain. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote skin health and well-being.
